entries.purpose: Two-model perplexity comparison (Hans et al. 2024). Scores a target against a scorer LLM + observer LLM pair; lower ratios indicate the target is more predictable under the scorer than the observer — a known signal for AI-generated text. v1 uses perplexity-ratio; v2 uses true cross-perplexity when tokenizers align.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal that's close to the published 95% AUC condition
You have access to a scorer + observer pair (default tinyllama + gpt2)
Target ≥ 50 tokens

do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (the conformal p-value is a membership statistic, not P(AI))
Your calibration scores are not exchangeable with the target (the guarantee fails)

optional: --calibration-positive nonconformity scores for a positive class (two-class mode)
required: --calibration nonconformity scores for the reference class (JSON list or newline-delimited)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: A split-conformal abstention layer over any signal: given operator-supplied calibration nonconformity scores and a target score, emit a distribution-free, finite-sample conformal p-value and a prediction set at coverage 1-alpha. Turns "the band reads uncalibrated" into "abstain, with a guaranteed error rate at this operating point." A methodology wrapper, NOT a detector; an empty or full prediction set is a licensed abstention. The p-value is NOT P(AI).
use_when: You have calibration nonconformity scores for a reference class and want a guaranteed-coverage abstention on a target score
You want to convert an uncalibrated signal into a finite-sample, distribution-free decision
You want a two-class prediction set (supply --calibration-positive)

do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance or authorship verdict (it produces a distance, not a call)
You want a morphology- or function-word-aware voice claim (use the English voice_distance for those)
Target and baseline are in different languages (cross-language comparison is meaningless)
Target is under 500 words (the length floor)

examples.description: Spanish voice distance vs a same-language baseline

required: --lang language code shared by target and corpus (provenance)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: A language-agnostic, parser-free stylometric distance between a target and a baseline corpus — character n-grams, punctuation profile, token-/sentence- length distributions, and script statistics. No spaCy, no English assumption; runs on any Unicode script with zero model dependencies. Honest about its ceiling: language-agnostic, NOT language-aware (no morphology, no function-word list), so it refuses morphology-dependent voice claims and refuses cross-language comparison (the --lang tag is provenance).
use_when: You want a voice distance for non-English prose, or any language without an English parser
You have a register-matched, same-language baseline corpus and a target in the same language
Target is >= 500 words

do_not_use_when: You only need to know one specific dep (just run the script and read the error)

use_when: First-run after installing the plugin
An ImportError or "module not found" error fires from any SETEC script
Confirming readiness before running a calibration or replication batch

examples.description: JSON envelope with a baseline of other chapters

optional: --baseline-dir DIR (other chapters / a character's prior dialogue)

entries.purpose: Per-character dialogue-voice profiling + cross-character divergence (Round 2 of pov_voice_profile, which profiles narration). Extracts quoted spans and tag-attributes them to speakers ("said X" / "X asked"); unattributed dialogue is bucketed separately and never force-attributed. Per character: contraction rate, mean turn length + variance, dialogue-tag verb diversity, vocative rate, interruption / trailing-punctuation rate, top function words + discourse markers. Reports a descriptive cross-character divergence matrix + a list of the lowest-divergence pairs — NOT a verdict. Refuses author-identity, AI-provenance, and quality inference.
use_when: Checking whether a multi-character draft keeps its characters voice-distinct in dialogue
Character-voice collapse is suspected (it surfaces in dialogue before narration)
You want a per-character spoken-register profile to pair with pov_voice_profile's narration layer

compute.cost_note: GPU fine-tune for calibration (RoBERTa-large ~355M); CPU inference with a calibrated model.

You have no same-corpus calibration (uncalibrated runs emit a score with no band)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Clean-room EditLens-style document-level edit-magnitude estimate — a RoBERTa-family regressor trained with MSE against a BERTScore-style similarity proxy between pre/post-edit pairs, framed as a SAME-CORPUS calibrated estimate with explicit OOD caveats, never an absolute "% AI". Uncalibrated by default (score only, no band); a calibrated model is operator-supplied via --model and applies only to the corpus it was calibrated on. The claim-license refuses an absolute %-AI / dosage claim, cross-corpus generalization, and per-sentence localization, and states the OOD-collapse caveat (degree estimation works in-distribution, collapses toward binary out-of-distribution).
use_when: You have an operator-calibrated model for a SPECIFIC corpus and want a same-corpus edit-magnitude estimate
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a document-level magnitude reading
You accept the in-distribution-only caveat

compute.cost_note: one small causal LM, ~0.6–2 GB weights; CPU works, GPU faster

do_not_use_when: You need a binary verdict — ships uncalibrated by default, no threshold
Target is out-of-distribution for the model, or may be in its training set (memorization biases the score)
The text has been paraphrased / surface-rewritten (curvature is paraphrase-sensitive)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch)
entries.purpose: Fast-DetectGPT conditional-probability curvature (Bao et al. 2024). Scores a target under a single causal LM and reports a curvature z-score: the standardized gap between the text's conditional log-probability and the distribution of conditional log-probs for alternative tokens sampled from the model's own per-position conditional distributions. Higher curvature means the text sits nearer a local maximum of the model's probability surface — a zero-shot signal for machine generation. Orthogonal to Binoculars' cross-perplexity ratio and DivEye's surprisal moments.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal distinct from Binoculars (single model, curvature not ratio)
You're stacking orthogonal Surface-5 signals (Binoculars + curvature + DivEye)
Target ≥ 50 words and roughly in-distribution for the scoring model

do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance call (this is explicitly NOT an AI signal)
You want a writing-quality judgment or a voice/authorship inference
Target is under 300 words (the length floor)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Descriptive phraseological-texture profile — density of common generic/stock phrases (clichés, filler transitions, hedge boilerplate, corporate idiom) from a small, user-extensible built-in list. NOT an AI detector and NOT a quality judgment: measurements only (no band/verdict), and the claim-license refuses AI-provenance / voice / quality inference.
use_when: You want a texture profile of how stock/generic a document's phrasing is
You're comparing formulaicity across documents or registers
Target is >= 300 words

examples.description: Build a preservation list from a writer's baseline

entries.purpose: Extract distinctive bigrams and trigrams from a writer's baseline corpus that are characteristic enough to flag during revision. Outputs a preservation list of phrases worth protecting across edits (Dunning's G² for bigrams, PMI for trigrams).
use_when: You're about to revise (or LLM-edit) a draft and want to know which phrasings to preserve
The writer has a baseline corpus of ≥ 50K words
You want to detect post-revision mimicry / cosplay (paired with mimicry_cosplay_audit)

compute.cost_note: embeddings; CPU-feasible but slow, GPU optional.

do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (this surface ships uncalibrated and refuses one)
You need values comparable across embedding models (PHD is embedding-model dependent)
Target is short (too few embedding units destabilizes the scaling fit)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Clean-room persistent-homology dimension (PHD) of a text's contextual-embedding point cloud — a topological discrimination signal reported in the literature as the single most orthogonal axis to perplexity / lexical signals (Tulchinskii et al. 2023). Human prose tends to a higher intrinsic dimension than AI prose. Emits the scalar PHD plus sample size and the embedding-model id; uncalibrated (no band, no verdict) and the claim-license refuses an AI/human verdict absent operator-supplied thresholds.
use_when: You want a geometry-based discrimination axis orthogonal to perplexity / lexical signals
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a topological reading
Target is >= 500 words (enough embedding units to fit the PHD scaling law)

entries.purpose: Descriptive narratorial-distance / free-indirect-discourse (FID) profile for developmental editing of literary fiction: per-window pronoun anchoring, perception/cognition-verb density, deictic anchoring (proximal vs. distal), evaluative-adjective density, a heuristic FID score, an overall close/distant distribution, and a distance trajectory across document position. Adjacent to pov_voice_profile but NOT a voice-identity, authorship, AI, or quality instrument — measurements only (no band, no verdict); FID detection is heuristic, not a parse of literary intent.

You're developmentally editing literary fiction and want an FID / narratorial-distance trajectory

entries.purpose: Replay SETEC's existing signals over PAN@CLEF obfuscation fixtures (unicode / paraphrase / lang_switch / short) and emit a per-(signal × obfuscation-class) robustness card: relative change plus a stable / degraded / collapsed tag per cell. You're prioritizing which SETEC signals need hardening against tokenizer / paraphrase / code-switch / short-text attacks

examples.description: Per-POV voiceprints + collapse verdict from a POV-tagged manifest

entries.purpose: Per-POV-character voiceprints for multi-POV fiction. Disaggregates a writer's baseline by the manifest's `pov` field and reports per-POV feature centroids, a pairwise POV voice-distance matrix (Burrows Delta + cosine), POV-vs-corpus-mean distance, top distinguishing features per POV, and a voice-collapse verdict (POV pairs whose pairwise Delta falls below a heuristic threshold).
use_when: Multi-POV fiction where you suspect the POV voices have collapsed into the writer's neutral default (e.g., a Blind Swap test fails)
You have a JSONL manifest tagging documents/chapters by `pov`, with ≥ ~3000 words per POV for stable centroids
You want to see which POV pairs are closest and which features distinguish each POV

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Strictly baseline-relative productive-roughness deviation profile — fragment rate, sentence-initial coordinating-conjunction rate, contraction rate, adjacent-word-repetition rate, interjection/aside rate, very-short- sentence rate. Reports each as the draft's deviation (draft, baseline mean ± sd, z) from the WRITER'S OWN baseline, never an absolute roughness or quality judgment.
use_when: You want to see how a draft's sentence-internal roughness deviates from a writer's own stable pattern
You have a writer-specific baseline corpus (their prior work) to compare against
You suspect AI editing has sanded off (or amplified) a writer's productive roughness

compute.cost_note: stdlib only (regex + counting); baseline comparison adds per-signal z-scores. No spaCy, no API costs.

do_not_use_when: Manuscript is below ~2000 words — the distributional signals are noisy at small sizes (the audit warns; the warning is the answer)
You want a calibrated AI/human verdict — the band calls and signals are heuristic (regularization bands against priors), diagnostic only
A first-draft fresh from the writer's hand — this audit catches regularization, so it has nothing to catch but the writer's natural rhythm

examples.description: Punctuation cadence vs. a personal baseline

entries.purpose: Punctuation rhythm + interruption-grammar audit. Treats punctuation as its own diagnostic dimension: per-mark density, sentence-final distribution, interruption grammar (parentheticals / em-dashes / appositives), punctuation bigrams, and a compression-fraction band call. Punctuation regularizes before vocabulary erases, so this is among the earliest signals to fire on AI-edited or house-styled prose.
use_when: As a follow-up to Layer A when lexical compression fired and you want to name what specifically was regularized at the punctuation layer
A line edit / copyedit "changed something" and you want the specific cadence shifts (best with --baseline-dir for z-scores)
A quick triage on AI-revised prose — punctuation cadence shifts early

do_not_use_when: You want any voice, authorship, or AI-provenance inference (this surface refuses it)
You're treating citation/link patterns as a voice signal (they are topic-bound)
Target is under 300 words (the length floor)

entries.purpose: Descriptive reference-ecology profile of a document — citation style and density (parenthetical / DOI / arXiv / et al.), footnote refs+defs, attribution constructions, quotation density, and external-link domain breadth. A thematic/register profile, NOT stylometry: emits measurements only (no band, no verdict) and refuses voice/authorship/AI inference, with an explicit topic-leakage caveat (reference ecology shifts with subject).
use_when: You want a profile of how a document cites, quotes, and links out
You're comparing reference/citation conventions across documents or registers
Target is >= 300 words

examples.description: Words over-represented vs. a personal baseline

entries.purpose: Vocabulary repetition diagnostic. Surfaces words over-represented in a target against the writer's own baseline (target/baseline frequency ratio) plus within-text clustering (max occurrences in a sliding window). Supports the vocabulary-restoration pass when Layer A flags lexical compression (MATTR / MTLD).
use_when: Layer A flagged lexical compression and you want the specific over-represented words to target in a vocabulary-restoration pass
You have a baseline corpus of the writer's own prior prose to compute the ratio against
You want to distinguish concentrated repetition (clustered) from distributed repetition (the cluster_max signal)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; no API costs at packet-build time

do_not_use_when: You don't have diagnostic JSON yet — run variance_audit / voice_distance first
You want the LLM to free-rewrite (this script's job is to constrain, not free)

examples.description: Build a packet from a variance audit

inputs.target: one or more diagnostic JSON files

entries.purpose: Translate Surface 1 / 2 diagnostic JSON into bounded revision-safe prompt packets. Reads variance_audit / voice_distance / idiolect / AIC outputs and classifies each signal as direct / translated / investigate-first / avoid-direct per the metric-targetability taxonomy. The aggregate divergence and overall distances are explicitly `avoid_direct` to resist metric-gaming.
use_when: You have Surface 1 / 2 diagnostics for a draft and want a structured revision plan
You're handing the draft to an LLM editor and need guardrails on what it can target
You want the post-check loop wired (before_after_restoration completes the cycle)

compute.cost_note: N billed LLM rewrite calls per audit (one per trial); cost scales with --n and target length. Operator supplies the LLM SDK + key.

do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (this surface is uncalibrated and ships no threshold)
You cannot pin a single judge model + prompt (the distance is meaningless without them)
You can't absorb per-call LLM cost (each trial is a billed rewrite; cost scales with --n)

required: --judge MODEL (operator-supplied LLM model id used to rewrite)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Raidar-style rewriting-invariance discrimination evidence: prompt an LLM to "rewrite to improve clarity," then measure how much it changed the text (edit-distance / token-overlap) averaged over N trials. Low change ⇒ more AI-like, high ⇒ more human-like. A paraphrase-robust axis orthogonal to perplexity. Uncalibrated: emits the distance + provenance only (no band, no verdict); strongly dependent on the judge model + exact prompt (both pinned in provenance). Clean-room implementation of the published idea (Mao et al., ICLR 2024) — no Raidar repo code/data vendored.
use_when: You want a discrimination signal that survives paraphrase attacks on perplexity detectors
You have an operator-supplied LLM (API key or local instruct model) to do the rewriting
You're stacking signals and want an axis orthogonal to surprisal / Binoculars

do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance call (this is explicitly NOT an AI signal)
You want a writing-quality judgment or a voice/authorship inference
You need a true phonetic transcription (this is an orthographic proxy, not phonemes)
Target is under 300 words (the length floor)

examples.description: Deviation vs the writer's own baseline

optional: --baseline-dir to report metrics as deviation from the writer's own baseline

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Descriptive sound-texture profile of prose — alliteration / assonance / consonance adjacency density and a consonant-class (plosive / fricative / sibilant / nasal / liquid / glide) profile — via an orthographic-onset proxy. The sonic layer the shipped suite (sentence-length variance only) is blind to. NOT a phonetic transcription, NOT an AI detector, NOT a quality judgment: measurements only (no band/verdict); the claim-license refuses AI-provenance / voice-identity / quality inference. Optional baseline-dir reports each metric as a deviation from the writer's own baseline.
use_when: You want a sonic-texture profile (alliteration / assonance / consonance / consonant-class) of a passage
You're developmentally editing literary prose where sound is part of voice
Target is >= 300 words

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; bootstrap is seeded + deterministic.

do_not_use_when: You want a ground-truth verdict (kappa measures agreement, not correctness)
You want to generalize beyond this (possibly non-representative) item set
You have fewer than 10 items (kappa is meaningless at very small N)

examples.description: CSV input with a larger bootstrap

inputs.target: JSONL (default) or CSV of per-item framework + human triage labels

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

entries.purpose: Measures the agreement between the framework's surfaced candidates and an operator's triage judgments on a labeled item set: confusion matrix, percent agreement, Cohen's kappa, the prevalence-and-bias-adjusted kappa (PABAK), and a seeded bootstrap CI on kappa. Closes the loop the framework keeps open on principle ("source triage is judgment work"). Measures CONCORDANCE, not correctness — it never decides who is right.
use_when: You have paired (framework, human) triage decisions and want the agreement as a number
You want to know whether the framework's candidate-surfacing matches expert reads on a register
You have at least 10 triaged items

compute.cost_note: scikit-learn + statsmodels for CIs; no API costs

do_not_use_when: The manifest is unvalidated — run manifest_validator first
The corpus is mixed-state (ai_status) without slicing — the harness refuses to pool
You want a single surface metric to be authoritative — the harness intentionally surfaces all surfaces
entries.purpose: Empirical validation harness for SETEC task-surface outputs. Computes FPR / TPR / AUC / AUPRC with Wilson / Clopper-Pearson CIs against a labeled manifest, enforces prompt-level grouping, and refuses to mix scores across task surfaces because the surfaces answer different questions.
use_when: You have a labeled corpus (with valid `corpus_manifest.jsonl`) and want to measure how SETEC signals behave on it
You're calibrating thresholds for an operator-specific corpus
You want CIs alongside point estimates (the framework's default)

You need a per-`language_status` ESL/L2 fairness slice — separate FPR/TPR/ROC for native vs non-native/L2/translated entries (on by default when any entry carries a non-`unknown` language_status; the harness refuses to publish a single aggregate FPR that pools native + non-native, surfacing the documented ESL false-positive failure mode rather than hiding it)

compute.cost_note: Tier 1 is stdlib + tokenization. Tier 2/3 need spaCy + en_core_web_sm. Tier 4 surprisal needs transformers + a small causal LM (default tinyllama, ~600 MB); the --tier4 flag opts in.

do_not_use_when: Target is ESL writing without a matched ESL baseline (the 2026-05-10 EditLens finding shows polarity inverts on ESL student writing)
You want a single AI/human verdict — variance_audit reports values, not a label
Target is under 200 words (Tier 1 length floor)

examples.description: Layer A + Layer B (with AIC-8/9 detectors)

entries.purpose: Layer A distributional diagnostic for AI-prose smoothing. Computes sentence-length variance, lexical diversity (MATTR / MTLD / Yule's K / Shannon), reading-level spread (FKGL SD), and POS-bigram / surprisal signals; reports per-signal values with operator-side band calls.
use_when: First pass on any prose where you want distributional evidence of mode-collapse
You have a baseline corpus (the writer's own prior work or a register match)
Target is ≥ 200 words for Tier 1; ≥ 500 for Tier 2/3; ≥ 2000 for Tier 4 surprisal

do_not_use_when: You don't have a writer-specific baseline (use variance_audit against a register baseline instead)
The baseline is mixed-state (some pre-AI, some AI-assisted) without slicing by ai_status
Target is under ~2000 words (Burrows Delta is noisy below that)
entries.purpose: Compare a target text against a specific writer's (or register's) baseline using classic stylometric feature families — Burrows Delta on function words, per-feature cosine distance, POS-bigram comparison. Answers "how far has this draft drifted from this writer's prior voice?"
use_when: You have a baseline of the writer's prior prose (≥ 20K words for tight bounds)
You suspect voice drift across drafts / time / register
You want to identify which feature families drove the drift (Delta + per-family cosine)

entries.purpose: time-drift surface (cathedral #6 sub-item).
use_when: TODO

compute.cost_note: loads a ~0.5–1.4 GB style encoder; CPU-feasible, GPU optional <2 GB VRAM

do_not_use_when: You need an identity verdict ("same person" / "different author") — the surface refuses it
You need an AI-vs-human call — this surface does not discriminate provenance
Windows are short (<~100 tokens) — style embeddings are fragile on short text; read the distribution, not single windows
You want a general-imposters win/loss proportion — use general_imposters.py (this is descriptive only)

examples.description: N-way embedding analogue (target vs candidate vs impostors)

entries.purpose: Same-author style-embedding surface. Embeds passages with a frozen style encoder (LUAR default; Wegmann optional via sentence-transformers) and reports the cosine-similarity DISTRIBUTION across windows — internal consistency (single), consistency with a baseline voice centroid (two-corpus), or where the target's candidate-similarity falls relative to an impostor pool (n-way, descriptive). Refuses any identity / AI verdict; ships PROVISIONAL with no threshold.
use_when: You want a learned-manifold complement to the scalar voice-coherence signals (voice_distance, idiolect_detector)
You want same-author-consistency evidence that needs no per-deployment training and no threshold
You want the embedding analogue of the general-imposters framing (target vs candidate vs impostor pool)

compute.cost_note: stdlib + spaCy (POS / dependency families need en_core_web_sm; --no-spacy drops them); no API costs

do_not_use_when: You want a target-vs-baseline drift score — that is voice_distance; voice_profile is descriptive, not diagnostic
You would share the output carelessly — a voice profile is voice-cloning-grade input (keep it private; the script refuses public output paths by default)

examples.description: Build a private voice profile from a baseline

entries.purpose: Produce a private, human-readable stylometric profile from a writer/register baseline corpus: per-family feature inventory (function words, character n-grams, POS trigrams, dependency n-grams) with each feature's baseline mean, SD, and coefficient of variation. This is the within-author reference (the aggregation voice_distance / idiolect_detector / mimicry_cosplay_audit build on), not a diagnostic verdict.
use_when: You want a durable within-author reference to anchor downstream voice-coherence work (voice_distance, idiolect_detector, mimicry)
You have a baseline corpus of the writer's prior prose (a directory or a manifest)
You want to see which feature families are most stable (low CV) for the writer
